title: Adverse Event Signal Mechanism
description: A GraphRAG backend for exploring FDA adverse event reports from the openFDA API. It ingests FAERS/AEMS reports, builds a Neo4j co-occurrence graph, indexes report embeddings in PostgreSQL with pgvector, and streams grounded answers with report-level provenance.

title: Trial Query Engine
description: A retrieval-augmented backend for querying clinical trial data. It ingests ClinicalTrials.gov records, retrieves source material for natural-language questions, streams grounded answers, and keeps source traceability through retrieved chunks.

title: Building Effective Agents
description: A hands-on exploration of building effective agents with LangGraph, inspired by Anthropic's guide on simple, composable agent patterns, routing, parallelization, orchestration, evaluation loops, and tool use.

title: Agentic Design Patterns
description: Exploring the frontier of AI autonomy. Inspired by Antonio Gulli, this notebook synthesizes the architectural patterns required to move beyond simple prompts toward multi-agent coordination and ethical autonomous frameworks.

title: Competitive Programming
description: A deep dive into algorithmic efficiency. This entry chronicles my journey through complex problem-solving, documenting the evolution of my logic from academic machine problems to optimized competitive solutions.

title: SWE Principles
description: The blueprint of quality code. I’ve consolidated my self-guided study of fundamental engineering principles here, creating a central knowledge base for the patterns and practices that separate 'coding' from 'engineering'.

title: Coding Interview University
description: My personal translation of John Washam’s famous roadmap. This isn't just a checklist; it's a rigorous documentation of my transition from a student to an interview-ready engineer, focusing on Computer Science depth.
